---
title: "2.2.3 Check digits"
subtitle: "Data Transmission P59-P61"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 2 Directory / 返回 Unit 2 目录](../../index.html)

## Check Digits

### Detecting data entry errors

A <span class="term">check digit</span> (校验数字) is the final digit included in a code; it is calculated from all the other digits in the code.

Check digits are used for <span class="mark">barcodes on products</span>, such as International Standard Book Numbers (ISBN) and Vehicle Identification Numbers (VIN).

::: {.tight-list}
- Check digits are used to identify <span class="mark">errors in data entry</span> caused by <span class="mark">mis-typing or mis-scanning</span> a barcode
- Different from transmission error checking (those detect bit errors during transmission)
:::

## Types of Errors Check Digits Detect

### Four types of data entry errors

Check digits can usually detect the following types of error:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="mark">An incorrect digit entered</span> — for example 5327 entered instead of 5307
- <span class="mark">Transposition errors</span> — where two numbers have changed order, for example 5037 instead of 5307
- <span class="mark">Omitted or extra digits</span> — for example 537 instead of 5307 or 53107 instead of 5307
- <span class="mark">Phonetic errors</span> — for example 13 (thirteen) instead of 30 (thirty)
:::

## ISBN-13 Check Digit

### Calculation 1 — Generation from 12 digits

The check digit in ISBN 13 is the <span class="mark">thirteenth digit</span> in the number. The algorithm generates the check digit from the 12 other digits:

1. <span class="mark">Add all the odd numbered digits</span> together
2. <span class="mark">Add all the even numbered digits</span> together and multiply the result by 3
3. Add the results from 1 and 2 together and <span class="mark">divide by 10</span>
4. Take the remainder; if zero use this value, otherwise <span class="mark">subtract the remainder from 10</span> to find the check digit

**Example: ISBN 9 7 8 0 3 4 0 9 8 3 8 2**
1. Odd: 9 + 8 + 3 + 0 + 8 + 8 = 36
2. Even: 3 × (7 + 0 + 4 + 9 + 3 + 2) = 3 × 25 = 75
3. (36 + 75)/10 = 111/10 = 11 remainder 1
4. 10 − 1 = 9 → <span class="mark">check digit is 9</span>

Full ISBN-13: 978 0 340 98382 <span class="mark">9</span>

## ISBN-13 Verification

### Calculation 2 — Re-calculation to verify

To check that an ISBN 13-digit code is correct (including its check digit):

1. <span class="mark">Add all the odd numbered digits</span> together, including the check digit
2. <span class="mark">Add all the even numbered digits</span> together and multiply the result by 3
3. Add the results together and <span class="mark">divide by 10</span>
4. The number is correct if the <span class="mark">remainder is zero</span>

**Verification: ISBN 9 7 8 0 3 4 0 9 8 3 8 2 9**
1. Odd: 9 + 8 + 3 + 0 + 8 + 8 + 9 = 45
2. Even: 3 × (7 + 0 + 4 + 9 + 3 + 2) = 75
3. (45 + 75)/10 = 120/10 = 12 <span class="mark">remainder 0</span>
4. Remainder is 0 → <span class="mark">number is correct</span>

## Modulo-11 Check Digit

### Calculation 1 — Generation from 7 digits

The <span class="term">modulo-11</span> method can have varying lengths of number, making it suitable for product codes or VINs:

1. Each digit is given a <span class="mark">weighting of 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2</span> starting from the left (weightings start from 8 since the number becomes eight-digit when the check digit is added)
2. The digit is <span class="mark">multiplied by its weighting</span> and then each value is added to make a total
3. The total is <span class="mark">divided by 11</span>
4. The remainder is <span class="mark">subtracted from 11</span> to find the check digit (if remainder is 10, check digit = 'X')

**Example: 7-digit number 4 1 5 6 7 1 0**
- Sum: (8×4)+(7×1)+(6×5)+(5×6)+(4×7)+(3×1)+(2×0) = 32+7+30+30+28+3+0 = 130
- 130/11 = 11 remainder 9
- 11 − 9 = <span class="mark">2 (check digit)</span>
- Result: 4 1 5 6 7 1 0 <span class="mark">2</span>

## Modulo-11 Verification

### Calculation 2 — Re-calculation to verify

To check that the eight-digit number is correct (including its check digit):

1. Each digit given weighting of <span class="mark">8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1</span> starting from the left
2. Digit × weighting, then <span class="mark">sum all values</span>
3. Divide total by <span class="mark">11</span>
4. The number is correct if the <span class="mark">remainder is zero</span>

**Verification: 4 1 5 6 7 1 0 2**
- Sum: (8×4)+(7×1)+(6×5)+(5×6)+(4×7)+(3×1)+(2×0)+(1×2) = 32+7+30+30+28+3+0+2 = 132
- 132/11 = 12 <span class="mark">remainder 0</span> → <span class="mark">number is correct</span>

## Classroom Check

### Understand check digits and verification

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="mark">check digit is the final digit in a code</span>, calculated from all other digits — detects data entry errors
- four types of errors detected: <span class="mark">incorrect digit, transposition, omitted/extra digits, phonetic errors</span>
- <span class="mark">ISBN-13: add odd digits + 3 × (sum of even digits)</span>, divide by 10, subtract remainder from 10 for check digit
- <span class="mark">verification: remainder is zero</span> when the calculation includes the check digit
- <span class="mark">modulo-11: weighted multiplication, divide by 11</span> — remainder 0 = correct; if generating, subtract remainder from 11 (10 = 'X')
- <span class="mark">different from transmission error detection</span> — check digits catch manual entry errors, not bit errors during transmission
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Automatic Repeat Requests (ARQ)

The next deck covers how retransmission protocols work when errors are detected.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 2 Directory / 返回 Unit 2 目录](../../index.html)
